diagrams.title: Array Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Shows contiguous memory allocation for a 1D array with 6 elements
diagrams.purpose: Helps students understand how arrays are stored in memory and how address calculation works

diagrams.title: Array Insertion at Beginning
diagrams.description: Visualizes the process of inserting element 10 at the beginning of array [20, 30, 40, 50]
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates why insertion at beginning has O(n) time complexity

diagrams.title: Binary Search Process
diagrams.description: Shows step-by-step binary search for element 23 in sorted array [2, 5, 8, 12, 16, 23, 38, 56, 72, 91]
elements: Initial: low=0, high=9, mid=4 (16) - 23>16, go right
Step 2: low=5, high=9, mid=7 (56) - 23<56, go left
diagrams.purpose: Helps visualize how binary search halves the search space each iteration

diagrams.title: 2D Array Row-Major vs Column-Major
diagrams.description: Compares how a 3x3 matrix is stored in memory for row-major and column-major orders
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies the difference between storage orders and helps with address calculation
